message:
Document async/event loop needs - copy from Bun next

IDENTIFIED: ThreadPool ≠ Event Loop

Current Status:
===============
✅ ThreadPool.zig copied (CPU-bound parallel work)
❌ Event loop missing (needed for Python asyncio)

The Issue:
==========
- ThreadPool: CPU-bound parallel computation
- Event Loop: Async I/O, timers, callbacks
- Python asyncio needs the EVENT LOOP, not ThreadPool!

What We Need from Bun:
======================

Priority 1: Event Loop (CRITICAL)
- src/bun.js/event_loop.zig
- src/bun.js/event_loop_ctx.zig
- src/io/io.zig

Priority 2: Async Primitives
- src/async/ (async utilities)
- src/bun.js/timer.zig (timers)

Priority 3: Platform Backends
- src/io/darwin.zig (kqueue for macOS)
- src/io/linux.zig (epoll for Linux)

Python asyncio API Needed:
===========================
- asyncio.run() - Run event loop
- asyncio.create_task() - Schedule tasks
- asyncio.sleep() - Async sleep
- asyncio.gather() - Concurrent execution

Why Bun's Code:
===============
✅ Written in Zig (no porting!)
✅ Production-tested (millions of users)
✅ High-performance (faster than Node.js)
✅ Cross-platform (macOS/Linux/Windows)

Estimated Effort:
=================
- Copy & adapt: 2-3 hours
- Implement asyncio API: 3-4 hours
- Testing: 2-3 hours
TOTAL: 7-10 hours (vs 40-60 from scratch!)

Action Plan:
============
1. Locate Bun's event loop files
2. Create packages/async_runtime/
3. Copy event_loop.zig + dependencies
4. Adapt for PyAOT's Python asyncio
5. Test with simple async Python code

Created .claude/BUN_ASYNC_TODO.md with full details.

This is the RIGHT approach - reuse Bun's proven async infrastructure! ✅
